heading: From JSON File to Rendered Page
items: You create a JSON file in backend/seeds/data/sections/ with a title, slug, description, and an array of content blocks
You add a $ref entry in backend/seeds/data/index.json pointing to your new file
You run `flask seed-db` which reads index.json, resolves all $ref pointers recursively, and writes the resolved data into the JsonDatabase as guide_sections.json
The seed command also derives a navigation list from the sections (id, title, slug, order, icon) and writes it as navigation.json
The Flask API serves the data at GET /api/guide/sections (list without blocks), GET /api/guide/sections/<slug> (full content), and GET /api/guide/navigation
The Next.js page dispatches the fetchSectionBySlug async thunk, which calls guideApi.getSectionBySlug(slug) using Axios
The Redux guideSlice stores the response in state.guide.currentSection
GuidePageLayout reads currentSection from the store and passes the blocks array to SectionRenderer
SectionRenderer maps over the blocks array and passes each block to BlockRenderer
BlockRenderer switches on block.type and delegates to the correct renderer component -- TextBlockRenderer, CodeBlockRenderer, TabsBlockRenderer, etc.
